name: Corteva, Inc.
business: Corteva is a leading global provider of seed and crop protection solutions focused on the agriculture industry. It serves farmers in approximately 110 countries through its Seed and Crop Protection segments.

exposures.sentence: Corteva is a leading global provider of seed and crop protection solutions focused on the agriculture industry.
The Seed segment develops and supplies commercial seed combining superior germplasm with advanced traits to produce high yield potential for farmers around the world.

The Crop Protection segment supplies products to protect crop yields against weeds, insects and disease, and that support overall crop health both above and below ground via nitrogen management and seed-applied technologies.
Its digital solutions provide data driven insights to help farmers optimize yield and profitability.
As a separate company, the Seed business plans to grow through opportunities in gene editing, biofuels, hybrid wheat, expanded crop offerings, out-licensing, and mergers and acquisitions.
As a separate company, the Seed business plans to grow through opportunities in gene editing, biofuels, hybrid wheat, expanded crop offerings, out-licensing, and mergers and acquisitions.
As a separate company, the Seed business plans to grow through opportunities in gene editing, biofuels, hybrid wheat, expanded crop offerings, out-licensing, and mergers and acquisitions.
The company is a leader in global herbicides, insecticides, nitrogen stabilizers, pasture and range management herbicides and biologicals and other nature-based products.
In 2025, substantially all of the company's branded soybean portfolio offers the Enlist E3 trait, in proprietary Corteva germplasm.

As a separate company, the Crop Protection business plans to grow through its differentiated, sustainable product offerings, including biologicals, with a focus on operational excellence and disciplined mergers and acquisitions.
On October 1, 2025, the company announced its intent to separate its Seed and Crop Protection businesses into two standalone, publicly traded companies.

catalysts.event: Announced intent to separate Seed and Crop Protection businesses into two standalone publicly traded companies via a tax-free spin-off
catalysts.sentence: On October 1, 2025, the company announced its intent to separate its Seed and Crop Protection businesses into two standalone, publicly traded companies, in a transaction that is intended to be a tax-free spin-off for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

catalysts.event: Approved a 6% increase in quarterly common stock dividend from $0.17 to $0.18 per share
catalysts.sentence: On July 29, 2025, the company's Board of Directors approved an approximately 6 percent increase in the quarterly common stock dividend from $0.17 per share to $0.18 per share.

catalysts.event: Crop Protection Operations Strategy Restructuring Program expected to be substantially complete by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The restructuring actions associated with these charges are expected to be substantially complete by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: Expected to contribute $40 million to pension plans (non-US) and $100 million to OPEB plans in 2026
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, Corteva expects to contribute approximately $40 million to its pension plans other than the principal U.S. pension plan, and approximately $100 million for its other post-employment benefit ("OPEB") plans.

catalysts.event: Crop Protection Operations Strategy Restructuring Program expected to achieve $180 million in run-rate savings by 2027
catalysts.sentence: The Crop Protection Operations Strategy Restructuring Program is expected to contribute to the company's ongoing cost and productivity improvement efforts through achieving an estimated $180 million of savings on a run rate basis by 2027.
